api: Box Platform REST API (https://api.box.com/2.0), OpenAPI 3.0 spec at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/box/box-openapi/main/openapi.json
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, scoped to the connector-configured folder (box_folder_id) and its immediate children only — this connector's config surface has no mechanism to enumerate arbitrary Box files/folders beyond that one folder, matching this bundle's original narrower purpose (folder-scoped file data extraction) as distinct from the sibling box bundle's account/enterprise-level scope. 2 read streams (files, file_details — a fan_out detail-GET over every id the files stream returns) and 1 write action (update_file) are implemented. Every other Box endpoint is out of scope for the reasons stated per-entry below; the file-content-management/binary-payload/account-admin surface this connector was never scoped to reach in the first place is unchanged from the pre-expansion bundle.

excluded.reason: OAuth2 client-credentials token exchange, not a syncable object stream

legacy's file_text stream has NO live HTTP implementation at all — Read() unconditionally returns an error outside fixture mode ('box-data-extract file_text live read requires fixture mode or a safe extraction service'); there is no real Box endpoint being called to port. Box's real extracted-text mechanism (GET /files/{file_id} with the x-rep-hints: [extracted_text] header, an async job-polling representation-generation flow) is a distinct, considerably more complex shape than a plain declarative GET this dialect cannot express without a Tier-2 StreamHook (async polling, a non-standard request header, and a representation-status retry loop) — ENGINE_GAP if pursued in a future increment, not silently approximated here. See docs.md's Known limits.
returns the raw file byte stream, not a JSON record; no declarative binary-payload shape exists in this dialect
irreversible file deletion; not a safe generic write target for a data-extraction-focused connector
file copy is a content-management action outside this connector's read/rename-focused scope

custom metadata instance listing on a file is a distinct catalog shape (arbitrary caller-defined key/value schema per enterprise) judged lower-value than file_details' fixed field set this pass; candidate for a future increment
per-file version history is a fan_out-of-a-fan_out shape (list files, then list each file's versions) judged lower-value than file_details this pass
